def: s.m. A ball, lump, &c. (see pinḍ); rising ground, an eminence
more: s. f. 1. Pace, step. 2. A rising ground, an eminence.
subs: s.m. The sweet or spanish potato, Batatos paniculata (cf. pinḍālu, s.v. piṇḍ)

def: s.f. Step, pace; way, path, track (see paiṅḍā).
s.m. The bottom (of a vessel, box, pot, &c.); the breech (of a cannon).
more: n. m. peṅdī ; Tir. penī , n. f. The bottom (of a vessel, box, etc.); the breech (of a cannon). peṅdī ke bal baiṭhnā , (Slang). v. n. 1. To sink bottomward; founder; flop down. 2. To be baffled, broken, vanquished, subdued.
n. m. A path (باتَ). paiṅḍ bharnā , v. n. To take an oath. Gangā kī māīṅ chār paiṅḍ bhar jā . Rus. paiṅḍā mārnā , E. v. n. 1. To stop one on the road. 2. To rob on the high road.

n. f. 1. A stand for water pitchers. 2. A pitcher ( ghaṛā ). Mārag apne chalā jā, mujh ko mat nā gher; Rājoṅ ghar paiṅḍā, merā jal ko hot aver . Stand off and clear the road, your own way go instead, Delay me not! the Rāja's pitcher on my head.
s. m. The bottom (of a vessel, box, pot, &c.).
s. m. Bottom (of a ship), breech (of a cannon: Harris ).

def: s.f. The bottom, &c. ( = pendā)
more: n. f. Mah. Wom. Fried sweet balls, given to lying-in-women, and to the bride and bridegroom as a strengthening nostrum a day before the marriage. Auroṅ ne ḍāl misrī gar pīṅḍīyāṅ, banāīṅ, Ham ne bhī guṛ maṅgā-kar bandhvāë til ke laḍḍū . Nazīr. While others' lips did candied cakes persuade, I too some jaggery got, til-laḍḍus made.
subs: v.n. To go down bottom foremost; to flop down; — to be overcome, be subdued.

def: s.f. Swinging; the exertion made by two people in a swing when they keep the swing in motion without the aid of others; — the ropes of a swing; — a kind of bird, Gracula chattareah (Buch.)
more: n. f. From H. pag foot. 1. The act of swinging very high by one's own exertions. 2. The ropes of a swing. Sāvan lāgā, tīj bhī āī, pīṅg ghalā de, rī mātā; Paṅch raṅg sūt raṅgā-kar begā ḍor baṭā de, rī mātā . Wom. Rus. song. August is come, and tīj is come, the swing put up now, ma! Gay cords of colors five now dye and twist for us, dear ma! pīṅg chaṛhānā, yā lenā , v. n. To swing very high.
1. s. f. The exertion made by people in a swing, when two persons keep the swing in motion without assistance from bystanders. 2. s. m. The name of a bird (Gracula chattareah: Buch. ).
subs: pīṅg lenā, v.n. To swing very high.

def: adj. (dakh.) Bandylegged ( Harris ): see پهينگڙا phengṛā.
s.f. Tenderness, fondness, affection.
v.t. corr. of pahinna, q.v. v.t. See پہننا pahinnā.
s.m. A pencil (lead or slate).
s. f. A market.
s.f. A stated or fixed market (syn. hāṭ), a market; market-day.
E. n. f. S. पण्यस्थान. A mart; market.
s.m. A tree; plant; shrub
more: s. f. A tree, a plant. پيڙلگانا peṛ lagānā, To plant trees. ¶ paiṛ, s. (dakh.) Sound of striking or of a blow.
wety: Inherited from Proto-Indo-Aryan *peḍ-. Cognate with Punjabi پیڑ (peṛ).

def: s.m. A lump, &c. (see pinḍa; and cf. peṛā).
wety: Inherited from Proto-Indo-Aryan *peḍ-. Cognate with Punjabi پیڑ (peṛ).

def: s.f. A kind of musical instrument.
more: n. m. S. पिण्ड a lump. 1. A ball of dough or kneaded flour. 2. A sweetmeat.

def: s.m. A ball or lump of leavened dough; — a kind of sweetmeat (made of curds).
more: s. m. 1. A kind of sweetmeat made with curds. 2. A globular mass of leaven prepared for baking.

def: s. (dakh.) See زراعت zirāʼaﻌt. ¶ pairāwā, s. (dakh.) Clothing.

s.f. The trunk of a tree; stubble (from which a second crop is obtained); the indigo plant after being once cut; a kind of betel leaf; a rate paid by cultivators to zamīndārs for the use of fruit trees.
s.f. A kind of sweetmeat ( = peṛā).
more: s. f. 1. A kind of sweetmeat made with curds (dimin. of پيڙا). 2. A kind of betel leaf. 3. The indigo plant after being once cut. 4. The trunk of a tree. ¶ paiṛī, s. f. A ladder, staircase, flight of steps.
s. (dakh.) Race, stock ( Harris ): see پيڙهي pīṛhī.

def: v.t. = peṛnā, q.v.
more: v. a. (dakh.) To sow, to plant ( Harris ).

def: v.t. To press, squeeze, crush, grind; to rack.
more: v. a. To press (oil by means of a mill), to squeeze, to rack.

def: s.m. The turquoise ( = pīroza).
s.m. The ocean; — the sun; fire.
more: n. m. peṭ belly. The pelvis; the belly between the navel and the pubes .

def: s.m. The belly below the navel, the pubes; locus ubi exoritur pubes.
more: s. m. The belly below the navel, pubes.

def: s.m. A turkey.
prediction; foretelling; forecast; prophecy
adj. & s.m. White, leprous; vile, ignoble; — leprosy.
alternative form of پیش گوئِی (peś goī)

ety: Old P., pes; Pehl. pesh; Z. para; S. para
def: s.m. Front, fore part; the front piece (in a native coat); (in Gram.) the vowel-point ُ (termed ẓamma by the Arabs); — adv. & postpn., In front (of, -ke), before (in time or place; — syn. āge); — adj. Promoted, advanced; respected; trusted; possessing influence
more: adv. Before. See اُگے (1.). pesh , n. m. 1. The front piece in a native coat. 2. The vowel point ('), termed by the Arabs zamma. pesh ānā , v. n. See under اُنا pesh-band , n. m. The belt that passes over a horse's breast to prevent the saddle slipping back; a martingal. pesh-band'ī , n. f. Anticipation; foresight; timely preparation. pesh-bīn , adj. Prudent; provident; circumspect. pesh-bīnī , n. f. Foresight; prudence. pesh jānā,—chalnā , v. n. 1. To go forth; advance. 2. To have effect; to be effective. Merī pesh nahīṅ chaltī, nahīṅ to dikhā dūṅ. pesh-khema , n. m. 1. The tent or other baggage sent on before; the camp equipage, tents, etc., sent on in advance of an army. 2. A forerunner; harbinger. Kātak jāṛe kā pesh-khemā hai. pesh-dastī , n. f. Being beforehand with; anticipation; forestalment. pesh-rau , n. m. 1. A forerunner. 2. A leader; guide; precursor; an advanced guard; a par…

1. adj. Before, in front, promoted, advanced, respected, confided in, possessing influence. 2. The vowel point (ُ). پيش ازاين pesh az īn, Before this. پيشامد pesh-āmad, s. f. Access, admittance. پيش آنا pesh-ānā, To treat, to behave, to negociate, to come before, to occur, happen, to present (itself). پيش بند pesh-band, s. m. The belt that passes over a horseʼs breast to prevent the saddleʼs slipping back. پيش بندي pesh-bandī, s. f. Foresight, timely preparation. پيش بين pesh-bīn, adj. Prudent, provident, wise. پيش بيني pesh-bīn-ī, s. f. Foresight, prudence. پيش جانا pesh-jānā, To go forth. پيش خيمه pesh-ḵẖaima, s. m. A tent or other baggage sent on before. پيش دست pesh-dast, adj. Anticipating, being beforehand, surpassing, one who excels; (subst.) deputy, manager or assistant (in office). پيش دستي pesh-dast-ī, s. f. The being or paying beforehand, anticipation, excelling, surpassing, pr…
subs: adv. Previous (to, -ke), before; — pesh az īn, adv. Previous to this, before this; formerly, heretofore ( = is-se āge, or is-se pahle)
s.f. Coming into the presence (of, kī), advance; access, admittance; advancement, elevation

v.n. To come before, step forward, to advance; to present (itself), to intervene, to arise, occur, happen, come to pass; to behave (with, -se, e.g. gustāḵẖī-se pesh āyā, 'he behaved insolently'), to treat; to deal (with), negotiate
adj. Looking to the future, provident, prudent
s.m. The belt that passes over a horse's breast to prevent the saddle's slipping back; a martingal
pesh ćalnā, v.n. To go forth, to advance; to gain reception (from, se), to pass, to answer; to be effective, have effect
s.m. A tent or other baggage sent on before; the camp equipage, tents, &c., sent on in advance of an army; — fore-runner, harbinger
s.m. Vestibule, porch, portico; verandah; gallery

adj. & s.m. Surpassing, excelling; outstripping; being beforehand, anticipating; — one who excels; an assistant; money paid in advance, earnest-money; — pesh-dastī, s.f. An outstripping, surpassing, excelling; pre-eminence, precedency, excellence; anticipation, the being beforehand (in, -meṅ), forestalling; paying beforehand; — pesh-dastī karnā, v.n. To make haste; to take the lead (in, -meṅ); to be beforehand (in), to anticipate
adj. & s.m. Early, first (fruit, grain, &c.); — first-fruits

adj. Making impression, impressive, forcible; successful, efficient, effectual (as a speech, admonition, &c.); — pesh-raft jānā, pesh-raft honā, v.n. To have effect, be effectual, produce an impression, to be heeded; to be successful, to succeed; — to take the lead, to go ahead
pesh-rau, s.m. One who goes before; leader; guide; precursor, fore-runner; party in advance; advance guard, van
s.f. The act of going before or preceding
s.f. Stepping before or forwards, advancing, advance; outstripping; anticipation; activity, alertness; aggression; — leadership, command
v.n. To go before, precede, go in advance; to advance, push forward; to outstrip; to anticipate; to take the first step, to commence; to be the aggressor
s.m. Deputy; manager, agent, foreman; assistant, mate; a native officer in a court next below the sarishtadār
s.f. Deputyship; agency, &c.; the office of a pesh-kār
v.t. To put or set before; to offer, present; to bring forward, adduce, submit, propose, represent
s.m. lit. 'What is first drawn'; first-fruits; — a present to a superior; a present to the ruling power on receiving an appointment, or on the renewal of a grant, lease, &c.
s.f. The foremost place, the front; vestibule, portico
s.m. A leader at prayer, an imām
s.m. lit. 'What is placed before'; exemplar, example, model; custom, habit, use; mode, manner; rule, regulation.

r: pesha, pesh, lit, pesha-wār, pesha-war, pesha-wālā
def: s.m. An artisan, craftsman, artificer; workman; tradesman.
more: n. m. Vocation; office; profession. See اُدم (3).
s. m. 1. Trade, profession. 2. Custom, habit, practice. (Much used in compos.). پيشه ور pesha-war, s. m. An artificer.

def: s.m. Urine, piss
more: n. m. S. प्रस्राव. 1. Urine; piss ( mūt ). Unke peshāb meṅ chirāg jaltā hai . Prov. Even the water he discharges reflects a burning light. (Said of the well-to-do). 2. ( Slang ). Semen; hence, a son; offspring. Jāne kis-kā peshāb hai? Prov. saying. Who knows whose spawn this is? peshāb karnā , v. n. To make water ( mūtnā ). Maiṅ tumhāre ghar peshāb karne bhī nahīṅ jātā . I would n't even make water in your house. peshāb kī rāh bahānā , v. a. To throw away money in wenching. peshāb nikalnā, yā khatā honā , v. n. To piddle in one's clothes through fright; hence, to be in fear of. Mujh se unkā peshāb nikaltā hai .
subs: peshāb-band, s.m. Stoppage or suppression of urine, strangury; — peshāb band honā, v.n. To suffer retention or stoppage of urine
v.n. To have the desire to pass urine, to want to piss
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پیشاب (pēšāb, literally “front-water”).

def: s.m. Grinding, crushing, pulverizing; — any apparatus for grinding or pounding, a hand-mill; a stone and muller; a threshing-floor; — a plant, a species of Euphorbia with three lobes, Euphorbia antiquorum (com. called tekāṅtā-sij).
s.f. The forehead, front, brow; fate, lot, destiny (as traceable in the lines of the forehead).
more: n. f. 1. The forehead ( māthā ); brow; front. 2. Fate ( qismat ); lot; destiny. 3. That part of a royal grant which is left blank, and which, when the deed is folded up, forms the outer fold; a title; heading.
s. f. The forehead, the brow.
s. f. Front, forehand (of a horse? Harris ).

def: s.f. A stone slab on which condiments, &c. are ground with a muller; mill, handmill; grindstone; millstone.
s.m. Name of a city on the north-west frontier of India (lit. 'an advanced post'; it was built by the emperor Akbar).
more: s. m. Name of a city, properly پيشاور pesh-āwar, built by Akbar. The word imports "an advanced post," as it is the next town to the Afg̠ẖāns.

def: adj. & s.f. Of or belonging to Peshaur, coming from or made in Peshaur; — a kind of slipper or shoe made in Peshaur; — a kind of rice.
more: s. f. 1. A kind of slipper made in Peshaur. 2. A kind of rice.

def: 1. An artisan; handycraftsman; workman; artificer. 2. A shopkeeper ( dukāndār ); tradesman. shāgird-peshā ; Illit. sāgar-peshā , n. m. Menial or house servants; domestics.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پیشه ور.

def: s. m. Martingal.
To make haste, to take the lead.
s. f. The foremost place, a portico.
s.f. Money given in advance, or on account; an advance (of money); earnest-money.
more: n. f. An advance on account.

def: n. f. A prediction.
s.f. The being put before, coming before (as a case before a judge), the being brought forward, or into the presence of; — lead, precedence; superior rank; — an advance ( = peshgī).
more: n. f. 1. Presence. 2. Trial; investigation. Āj muqadme kī peshī hai .
s. f. 1. An advance. 2. Prece dence, superiour rank.

def: adj. Former, prior, anterior, ancient; anticipated; — s.m. The morning
more: adj. Anteriour, ancient, prior, former. s. m. The afternoon.

def: s.m. pl. Those who have gone-before, the ancients.
= P پيشکش pesh-kash, See s.v. pesh.
more: n. m. 1. A foreman; agent; manager. 2. A native officer in a Court next below the sarishtedār .
s. m. A deputy, agent, assistant, a minister, a manager, foreman.

def: n. f. Agency; the office of a pesh-kār .
= P پيشکار pesh-kār, See s.v. pesh.
more: n. f. lit . What is first drawn; first fruits. 1. A present to a superior. 2. A present ( salāmī ) to the ruling power on receiving an appointment or assignment of revenue, or on the renewal of a grant, lease, etc.
s. m. A present, a tribute.

def: adv. Before, prior (to, -ke, or -se); formerly; sooner; heretofore; ago; further; beyond.
more: adv. Before, prior, sooner, formerly, heretofore, ago.

def: s.m. Exemplar, model; guide, leader; foreman; the head of a party; high-priest; the chief of a religious sect; a moral or religious instructor; — the first executive officer (among the Marhaṭṭas)
more: n. m. 1. A leader; guide. 2. A priest; a moral or religious instructor.
s. m. 1. A leader, a guide, foreman. 2. A title of the Mahārātta minister. پيشوالينا peshwā lenā, To meet and to receive (a friend or visitant).
subs: v.t. To follow as a model; to take as a guide, &c.
(-kā), To meet and receive (a friend or visitor).

def: s.f. Leadership; high-priestship; guidance; precedence, supremacy; the meeting and reception of a guest or visitor
more: n. f. 1. Guidance; precedence; leadership; supremacy. 2. The escort and reception of a visitor or guest. peshvāī karnā , v. n. To escort; receive; welcome.
subs: (-kī), To meet, receive, welcome; to escort.

def: s. f. The meeting and conducting a guest or visitor, the duty of chief priest, guidance, supremacy.
s.f. A full-dress gown (reaching a little below the knee, — especially such as is worn by a bride when she leaves for her husband's home, and also by dancing girls).
more: s. f. A gown, a female dress.
s. m. (dakh.) Chief, leader: see پيشوا peshwā.

def: s.f. A piece, ball, or lump of flesh or meat; a sheath, scabbard; the egg of a bird; spikenard, Valeriana jatamansi; a blown bud.
adj. Leprous.
(dakh.) To curry favour ( Harris ).
adv. For the sake of the stomach
more: s. m. 1. The belly. 2. The womb, pregnancy. 3. The bore (of a gun, &c.). 4. Cavity, capacity. پيٿ آنا peṭ ānā, To be purged. پيٿ باندهنا peṭ bāndhnā, To eat less than oneʼs appetite demands. پيٿ بڙهانا peṭ baṛhānā, To eat voraciously; to encroach on the share or rights of another. پيٿ بهر peṭ-bhar, adv. Bellyful. پيٿ بهرنا peṭ bharnā, 1. To fill oneʼs belly. 2. To be satisfied. پيٿ پالنا peṭ pālnā, 1. To be able to live decently. 2. To be selfish. پيٿ پاني هونا peṭ pānī honā, To be violently purged, to have watery stools. پيٿ پکنا peṭ paknā, To burst with laughter. پيٿ پوسو peṭ-posū, or پيٿ پوسوا peṭ-posūʼā, adj. Glutton. پيٿ پونچهن peṭ-ponchhan, The last child of a woman. پيٿ پهولنا peṭ phūlnā, To burst with laughter. پيٿ پيٿهـ ايک هونا peṭ pīṭh ek honā, To be greatly emaciated. پيٿ پيٿهـ بتانا peṭ pīṭh batānā (i. q. عاجزي کرنا āﻌjiz-ī karnā ) To implore (?). پيٿ جاري هونا peṭ jārī honā…
subs: peṭ-ārthī, adj. & s.m. Desirous for the stomach; greedy, gluttonous; epicurean; — a glutton; an epicure
v.n. To eat less than the appetite demands, to stint oneself of food
v.n. To eat voraciously; — to encroach on the share or rights of another
v.n. The stomach to rumble; to suffer from borborygm
adj. (f. -ī), Having the stomach full; satisfied; — rich, wealthy, well off, independent; consequential, proud, conceited
adj. & s.m. Enough to fill the stomach; a bellyful
v.n. To fill the belly; to be satisfied; to be surfeited
v.n. To pamper the stomach; to be selfish; — to contrive to live decently; to live from hand to mouth
adj. & s.m. Gluttonous, greedy, selfish; — a glutton; a selfish person
v.n. To have loose bowels; to be violently purged; to have watery stools; — to be white-livered, be frightened; to be confounded
v.n. lit. 'To hold the stomach and run'; to fly in great agitation or distress
v.n. lit. 'To walk about holding the stomach'; to wander about in a state of agitation or distraction, to be distracted
peṭ-posūʼā, adj. & s.m. Gluttonous, greedy; a glutton ( = peṭ-ārthu; peṭ-pālū)
peṭ-poćhan, peṭ-poṅćan, s.m. A woman's last child
v.n. To rend or tear the bowels; — to be impatient
v.n. The belly to burst; — to be impatient; to be bursting with envy, &c.
v.n. The belly to swell; to be in the family way; to be bursting (with laughter, &c.)
v.n. To beat the belly; to cry out with hunger; — to evince great impatience
(or peṭ-piṭh) batānā, v.n. To evince extreme weakness or want; to act submissively
(or peṭ piṭh) ek honā, v.n. lit. 'The belly and back to meet'; to be very emaciated
peṭ jhaṛnā, peṭ ćalnā, peṭ ćhūṭnā, v.n. To be purged; to have a diarrhœa or flux ( = peṭ pānī honā)
v.n. lit. 'To shew the belly'; to complain of poverty and hunger; to beg for food
v.n. To procure abortion; bring on a miscarriage
v.t. To get with child, cause to be pregnant
v.t. To bind (food, &c.) to the belly
v.n. One's teeth or claws to become visible; to shew one's claws; — to go beyond bounds
peṭ-se īṅṭ bāṅdhnā, peṭ-se kāṭ-kī roṭī bāṅdhnā, v.n. To bind a tight bandage, or a brick, or a cake-shaped board, to the stomach to withstand the craving of hunger
v.n. To be carried (in a conveyance), or to ride, without being jolted

(-kā), To starve or pinch (oneself); to deprive (one) of dues, livelihood, &c.; to stop the wages (of); to reduce the allowance or stipend (of); — peṭ-kā ṭūṭā, adj. Starving, famished, famine-stricken
s.m. One subject to pain in the stomach (from over-feeding); — a destitute or starved person, a poor wretch; — a wretch who will do anything for the sake of his stomach
peṭ-kā kuttā, adj. As a hog, or a dog, in respect of (his) stomach; greedy as a hog, &c.
s.m. One who works for bare subsistence; a hireling, mercenary
adj. (f. -ī), Light of stomach; light, frivolous; gossiping, babbling; unable to keep a secret
s.f. Fire of the stomach; hunger; — maternal affection
v.n. To appease hunger; — peṭ-kī angār, s.f. Violent craving of the stomach (for food); — violent emotion
peṭ-ke pāʼoṅ bāhar nikālnā = peṭ-se pāʼoṅ bāhar nikālnā
adv. For the sake of the stomach; for food
(-kā), To cause or procure abortion ( = peṭ ḍalnā)
v.n. To miscarry (a woman); — s.m. Miscarriage
peṭ guṛgurānā, v.n. To have a rumbling of the stomach ( = peṭ bolnā, q.v.)
v.n. The stomach to sink in from want of food; to be starving with hunger
(apnā), To subdue (one's) appetite or hunger; to mortify (oneself); to stab (oneself) in the belly; — to screen, cloak, hide, excuse (a child from maternal affection)
s.f. A woman who works for her daily bread; a woman who prostitutes herself for bread or for money; a prostitute, a harlot
v.n. To have the stomach rubbed
v.n. To have a twisting pain in the stomach (from excessive laughter, &c.)
v.n. To keep a thing, &c. to oneself; — peṭ-meṅ pānī honā, v.n. = peṭ pānī honā, q.v.
v.n. To be very sly; to entertain evil designs
v.n. To get into the stomach (of, -ke); — to pass the lips (of); — to conceive
(-ke), To worm oneself into the secrets (of); to worm oneself into the good graces (of); to become intimate (with)
v.n. To have an old head on young shoulders
(-ko), To stow away in the stomach; to keep (a matter) secret, or to oneself; to keep back; to suppress
v.t. To gulp down; to endure, to have patience
v.n. To have a griping pain in the stomach, to have an inclination to stool.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit पेट (peṭa, “bag, basket”), from the root verb पिट् (piṭ, “to collect or heap together”).

def: s.m. Belly; round, girth; breadth, width, diameter, distance across; slackness or curvature (of a string or rope hung loosely between two points); the middle (of a page); the channel (of a river); a round or total sum; — adv. In round numbers, about.
s.f. A basket; a large basket.

n. m. 1. The adipose membrane. 2. Slackness or curvature (of a kite-string, tent-ropes, etc.) 3. The middle (of a page). 4. The channel of a river. 5. Distance. 6. A total sum. peṭ'ā , adv. About; in round numbers.
s.m. A covered wicker-basket for holding clothes, books, &c.; — a multitude.
adj. Big-bellied, gorbellied.
s.m. See piṭārā.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. peṭ. adv. For the sake of the stomach

v.n. To eat less than the appetite demands, to stint oneself of food
v.n. To eat voraciously; — to encroach on the share or rights of another
v.n. The stomach to rumble; to suffer from borborygm
adj. (f. -ī), Having the stomach full; satisfied; — rich, wealthy, well off, independent; consequential, proud, conceited
adj. & s.m. Enough to fill the stomach; a bellyful
v.n. To fill the belly; to be satisfied; to be surfeited
v.n. To pamper the stomach; to be selfish; — to contrive to live decently; to live from hand to mouth

v.n. To have loose bowels; to be violently purged; to have watery stools; — to be white-livered, be frightened; to be confounded

peṭ-poćhan, peṭ-poṅćan, s.m. A woman's last child
v.n. To rend or tear the bowels; — to be impatient

v.n. lit. 'To shew the belly'; to complain of poverty and hunger; to beg for food
v.n. To procure abortion; bring on a miscarriage

v.n. To be carried (in a conveyance), or to ride, without being jolted

s.m. One subject to pain in the stomach (from over-feeding); — a destitute or starved person, a poor wretch; — a wretch who will do anything for the sake of his stomach
peṭ-kā kuttā, adj. As a hog, or a dog, in respect of (his) stomach; greedy as a hog, &c.
s.m. One who works for bare subsistence; a hireling, mercenary
adj. (f. -ī), Light of stomach; light, frivolous; gossiping, babbling; unable to keep a secret

v.n. To appease hunger; — peṭ-kī angār, s.f. Violent craving of the stomach (for food); — violent emotion
peṭ-ke pāʼoṅ bāhar nikālnā = peṭ-se pāʼoṅ bāhar nikālnā
adv. For the sake of the stomach; for food
(-kā), To cause or procure abortion ( = peṭ ḍalnā)
v.n. To miscarry (a woman); — s.m. Miscarriage
peṭ guṛgurānā, v.n. To have a rumbling of the stomach ( = peṭ bolnā, q.v.)
v.n. The stomach to sink in from want of food; to be starving with hunger

s.f. A woman who works for her daily bread; a woman who prostitutes herself for bread or for money; a prostitute, a harlot
v.n. To have the stomach rubbed
v.n. To have a twisting pain in the stomach (from excessive laughter, &c.)
v.n. To keep a thing, &c. to oneself; — peṭ-meṅ pānī honā, v.n. = peṭ pānī honā, q.v.
v.n. To be very sly; to entertain evil designs
v.n. To get into the stomach (of, -ke); — to pass the lips (of); — to conceive
(-ke), To worm oneself into the secrets (of); to worm oneself into the good graces (of); to become intimate (with)
v.n. To have an old head on young shoulders
(-ko), To stow away in the stomach; to keep (a matter) secret, or to oneself; to keep back; to suppress
v.t. To gulp down; to endure, to have patience
v.n. To have a griping pain in the stomach, to have an inclination to stool.

s.f. A box.

more: E. n. f. S. पीयुस Coagulated milk in the udders of a newly-calved cow.

def: adj. & s.m. Suitable or fit to be taken or drunk, drinkable; — water, milk, any drink.
part. adj. Sifting (used in comp.).

more: v. n. To shoot forth (as, a plant).

more: v. a. See phabnā , v. n.

def: adj. (Old H.) = phabīlā, q.v. part. adj. (f. -ī), Becoming, fit, suitable, proper, pertinent; — phabte, adv. Fittingly, pertinently, àpropos.
adj. (f. -ī), Becoming, fit, &c. = phabtā, q.v. part. adj. (f. -ī), Becoming, fit, suitable, proper, pertinent; — phabte, adv. Fittingly, pertinently, àpropos.

v. n. To become, befit, fit.

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Becoming, fit, suitable, proper, pertinent; — phabte, adv. Fittingly, pertinently, àpropos.

more: n. f. A name given in jest which sticks to one; chaff. Kaṭoṛī gāch kī pahnī jo ho, guīyān, kahūṅ phabtī, Anāroṅ par lagāyā ā-ke ek makṛī ne jālā haī . Should I upon the gauze your breasts encase a jest essay, I'd say a spider's web on twin pomegranates bursting lay. phabtī kahnā, yā uṛānā , v. a. 1. To chaff. 2. To put to the blush.
(from پهبنا) s. f. Ornament, conjecturing what a person is by his dress. پهبتي کـﮩنا phabtī kahnā, v. a. To say what is conjectured by oneʼs dress.

def: (dakh.) See اخير aḵẖīr.

more: v. n. 1. To break out or erupt (as pimples or postules). 2. To throw out shoots; to grow luxuriantly. Darakht phir phadphadāī āyā .

def: s.m. A red powder thrown over one another by the Hindūs at the holī festival; the act of throwing coloured powders at the time of the Holī; the sports of the Holī
more: n. m. S. फाल्गुन The festivities of the Holī. Ādhe gāoṅ Diwālī, ādhe gāoṅ phāg . Prov. One half the village plays Divālī , the other half Holī . (Half the village is at feud with the other half). phāg khelnā , v. n. To play, or enjoy the Holī . 1. Jo jīve so khele phāg . Prov. Who live unto this day will Holī play. 2. Jamā lage sarkār kī, aur Mirzā kheleṅ phāg . Prov. The Government collections in to-day, the Mirza. makes grand Holī's holiday. laṅgoṭī meṅ phāg khelnā , v. n. To be jolly over little.
s. m. 1. Red powder (sprinkled in the time of the Holī ). 2. The act of throwing coloured powders in the Holī, the gambols of the Holī.
subs: v.n. To take part in the gambols of the Holī, to play holī.

s. m. The name of the 11th month, the full moon of which is near pūrvaphalgunī. February-March.

Holī Mauj. In hot pursuit, in struggle and fight, my clothes may tear, my limbs may ache, I will my Holi sport have out with you, my maiden shame I'll stake.
s. m. 1. The same as هولي holī. 2. Presents made during the holī holidays.

v.t. To cause (a flag) to wave or fly; to wave, to fly, to flap (a flag).
v. n. To fly (as, a flag in the air), to flutter.

ety: caus. of phailnā; — phailā˚ = phail+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि caus. increment
def: v.t. To stretch, stretch out, extend, expand; to open wide, distend, dilate; to enlarge, increase; to lengthen, draw out, deploy; to spread, spread out; to scatter, strew; to diffuse, spread abroad, publish, proclaim, propagate; to spread over, to distribute, apportion; to work out (a sum), to compute, calculate.
more: v. a. S. प्रसृ to stretch forth. 1. To stretch; stretch out; extend; distend; expand; deploy. 2. To lengthen; prolong; draw out; spin out. 3. To multiply; add to; increase; augment; enlarge ( baṛhānā ); develop. 4. To enlarge; dilate; amplify; spin out. 5. To scatter; strew; sow; disperse. Billī khāëgī nahīṅ tau phailā to zurūr degī . Prov. What the cat wont eat she is sure to scatter. 6. To diffuse; circulate; spread abroad; make known; publish; propagate. 7. To distribute ( bāṅṭnā ); deal out; apportion. 8. To calculate; work out; count; estimate. muṅh phailānā . v. n. To open one's mouth wide.

(trans. of پهيلنا) v. a. To spread, scatter, expand, diffuse, widen, stretch out, extend, distend, dilate, publish, proclaim, to branch out. پانوپهيلانا pāṅw phailānā, see under پانو pāṅw. هاتهـ پهيلانا hāth phailānā, To beg.

def: n. m. 1. Extent; length; breadth; dimension. 2. Expansion; extension; spread; enlargement; multiplication. 3. Growth; enlargement; development. 4. def: v.n. To be spread, be expanded, be dilated; to be diffused, be scattered, be dispersed; to spread, expand, extend, stretch or reach out; to grow, increase (in size, &c.); to spread abroad, become public; to rise in one's demands or requirements, be exacting; to become pressing, to insist, persist
more: v. n. See پهيلانا v. a. 1. To grow luxuriantly; come on well; flourish. Kyā peṛ phailā hai! 2. To encroach upon; exaggerate one's demands. Muṅh-māṅgī murād miltī dekh-ke phailne lage . 3. To insist on; make a point; to press; persist. Muft kī dekhī aur phailne lage .
v. n. 1. To be spread, to spread. 2. To be expanded, dilated, diffused, scattered or dispersed. 3. To become publick.

s.m. Fruit; produce, product, crop, yield; offspring, children; return, requital, recompense; gain, profit, advantage; result, effect, consequence; inference, corollary; (in Arith.) the quotient; the answer (of a problem, riddle, &c.); the area or superficial contents (of a figure); the fulfilment (of an omen); — the iron head (of a spear, arrow, &c.); the blade (of a sword, knife, &c.); a ploughshare; a shield; a kind of fragrant berry and drug (commonly called kakoli); a nutmeg
more: n. m. S. फल to bear fruit. 1. Fruit in general (melon especially); a crop; produce; yield; harvest. Phar na pharī, bagaichā ke nāoṅ! Bhoj. Prov. Nor fruit nor vegetables, a garden called! 1. Phūl āë haiṅ, to phal bhī āëgā . Prov. When you see the blossom you may expect the fruit. 2. Phal khānā āsān nahīṅ hai . Prov. saying. 2. Children ( aulād ). 3. The answer (of a riddle, problem, dream, etc). 4. Gain; profit; benefit; reward. 5. Result; effect; issue; consequence. 6. The iron head of a spear or arrow; a blade (of a knife or sword); a ploughshare. Ek achambā dekho chal, sūkhī lakṛī lāgo phal; Jo koī us phal * ko khāë † , peṛ chhoṛ kahiṅ aur na jāë . Riddle. Ans. Barchhī a spear. Come all, a wonder you shall see, A fruit upon a dried-up tree! Whoe'er this fruit may chance to eat, Will one be with the tree complete. 7. ( Arith .) Quotient ( khārij qismat ). phal ānā , v. n. To bear, or b…

s. m. 1. Fruit; effect, advantage, children, progeny. پهل پانا phal pānā, To reap the reward of (good or bad actions). پهل پهلاري phal phalārī, Fruits of various kinds. پهل دار phal-dār, adj. Fruitful. 2. The iron head of a spear or arrow, &c. 3. A blade (of a sword, &c.).
subs: (phala+apekshā), s.f. Regard to results, expectation of consequences
(phala+āśī), adj. & s.m. Feeding or living on fruits; — any creature that lives on fruits
v.n. Fruit to appear; to bear fruit
s.m. Taking a slight repast, eating fruits, parched corn, &c. for a meal; — a repast of fruits, &c.
phal-bujhauwal, s.m. A boys' game (called also mankelā: 'think of a number; double it; add ten to it; take five from it, &c.: how much remains? Twenty-one:' the number was eight)
s.m. Enjoyment of consequences; possession of rent or profit, usufruct
adj. & s.m. Enjoying fruits or consequences; receiving profits; — one who reaps consequences, profits, &c.

s.m. The ripening of fruit; the fulness of consequences; — the tree and fruit Carissa carandas (com. called karaundā); — phal-pākāntā (i.e. pāka+antā), s.f. An annual plant which dies after bearing fruit
(-kā), To reap the fruits or consequences (of), to reap the reward (of), to get one's deserts
part. adj. Obtaining the consequences (of actions), rewarded, recompensed; punished
s.f. Obtaining (the desired) fruit or result; enjoying the consequences (of actions)
phal-phalālī, phal-phalyārī, phal-phalairī, s.f. Fruits of various kinds, fruitage
s.m. lit. 'Full of kernels'; the common citron
s.m. The fruit-bearing tāṛ, the female palm
phala-traya, s.m. 'Fruit-triad'; three sorts of fruit collectively (the fruit of the vine with those of Grewia asiatica or Xylocarpus granatum and Gmelina arborea); the three myrobalans
s.m. The bark of the Indian fig-tree (ground and eaten with curds by way of penance)

phalad, phal-dātā, phal-dātr̤i, phal-dāʼī, phal-dāyak, adj. & s.m. Yielding or bearing fruit; bringing profit or gain; yielding a result or consequence; giving a reward; fertile; profitable, advantageous, useful, efficacious; — one who rewards; a benefactor; — phala-da, phalad, s.m. wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi پھل (phl /⁠phal⁠/) (c. 1518), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀨𑀮 (phala), from Sanskrit फल (phála), borrowed from Proto-Dravidian *paẓam (“ripe fruit”).

def: s.m. A noose, &c. ( = phāṅd); an impediment; — the share of the bākhar plough; — a die for playing with.
more: n. m. S. पाश A noose.
s. m. 1. A noose. 2. An impediment.
s. m. (dakh.) Lot, a die (for playing; پهانسي phānse, Dice: Harris ).

ety: caus. of phaṅsnā; — phaṅsā˚ = phaṇsāw˚ = Prk. फंसावे(इ) or फंसाव(इ) = S. स्पृश+आपि
def: v.t. To cause to be snared or noosed; to cause to be caught or entrapped; to cause to be involved; to ensnare, entrap, atch; to inveigle, entangle, involve (in), to implicate.
more: v. a. S. पश् to bind, or S. स्पर्श touching, Pr. phanso . 1. To entangle; involve; implicate. 2. To ensnare; entrap; inveigle.
(caus. of پهنسنا) v. a. To cause to be ensnared, caught or involved (in), &c. to entrap.

def: s.m. Entanglement; an ensnaring or entrapping business or affair; a sticking place (as a muddy road, a bog, or fen); a narrow place.
more: n. m. See پهندا and ارَاس

def: s.f. Noose, loop, snare; halter; strangulation, hanging, death by hanging: phāṅsī pānā, phāṅsī paṛnā, v.n. To be hanged
more: n. f. 1. A snare; noose; halter. Jhāṅsī gale kī phāṅsī, Datyā gale kā hār, Lalatpūr na chhāḍye jab tak mile adhār . In Jhāṅsī you're strung by the neck, and Dattya's a noose you cant slip, The place to live is Lalatpur while bread you've in your scrip. 2. Hanging; execution; strangulation; death by hanging. phāṅsī pānā, yā chaṛhnā , v. n. To be hanged, or executed. Phr.—To be hanged by the neck; to come to the gallows. phāṅsī denā, yā chaṛhānā , v. a. To hang; strangle; suspend by the neck; punish capitally; execute. phāṅsī kā khambā . The gallows. phāṅsī-gar , n. m. A strangler. A robber and murderer who strangles his victims by throwing a turban or cloth round their necks. phāṅsī dene-wālā , n. m. A hangman.

s. f. A noose, a loop, halter; strangulation. پهانسي پڙناياپرنا phāṅsī -paṛnā or -parnā, v. n. To be hanged. پهانسي دينا phāṅsī denā, v. a. To strangle, to hang. پهانسي لگانا phāṅsī lagānā, v. a. To kill oneʼs self by strangling.
subs: v.n. To have the noose put on the neck; to mount the scaffold, to be hanged
phāṅsī-kā denā, v.t. To strangle, to hang
phāṅsī-gar, s.m. A strangler; a robber and murderer who strangles his victims by means of a turban or cloth, a ṭhag.

def: s.m. A foot-pad who strangles travellers, a ṭhag ( = phāṅsī-gar, q.v.); a hangman.
more: (from پهانسي q. v.) s. m. A footpad who strangles passengers or travellers.

def: v.n. = phaṅsnā, q.v.; — v.t. = phaṅsānā, q.v.
more: v. a. See پهنسنا v. n.
1. v. a. To noose, to ensnare, to entrap, to throttle, to strangle, to choak. 2. v. n. To be impeded, to stick (as, in mud); to be perplexed.

ety: phaṅs˚ = Prk. फंस(इ) = S. स्पृश(ति), rt. स्पृस्
def: v.n. To be taken in a noose, &c.; to be noosed, be ensnared, be entrapped; to be suspended (as a vessel with a noose round it); to be caught; to be imprisoned; to be entangled; to stick (as in mud, or in a narrow passage); to be impeded; to be embroiled, be involved (in difficulties, calamity, &c.).
more: v. n. See اُلجهنا (5 to 15). 1. Dekhan ke to atīt haiṅ, bisvā se rahe phaṅs, Māthe tilak lagāë haiṅ, mālā gal meṅ das . Dohā. A sainted devotee to see, by harlot's wiles he's caught, His forehead stained with pious paint, ten strings of beads a lot. 2. Haṅsī aur phaṅsī . Prov. She laughs—she is mine. ('He translates honesty into English'). 1. To be hung, suspended. Loṭā phaṅsnā . 2. To stick, as in mud or in a narrow passage. Chūṛī phaṅstī hai . 3. To be entangled, embroiled, involved (in a calamity, etc.).
v. n. To be entangled, to stick, to be involved (in calamity, &c.), to be ensnared, noosed or entrapped, to be caught. See پهسنا phasnā.

def: s.f. = phāṅsī, q.v. s.f. Noose, loop, snare; halter; strangulation, hanging, death by hanging: phāṅsī pānā, phāṅsī paṛnā, v.n. To be hanged
subs: v.n. To have the noose put on the neck; to mount the scaffold, to be hanged
phāṅsī-kā denā, v.t. To strangle, to hang
phāṅsī-gar, s.m. A strangler; a robber and murderer who strangles his victims by means of a turban or cloth, a ṭhag.

def: v.t. = phaṅsānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to be snared or noosed; to cause to be caught or entrapped; to cause to be involved; to ensnare, entrap, atch; to inveigle, entangle, involve (in), to implicate.
more: (caus. of پهانسنا) v. a. To cause to noose, strangle, &c.

def: s.m. A village register.
s. (dakh. i. q. شاخ) Branch, bough.
s.m. = fānūs, q.v. s.f. A pharos, lighthouse; a lantern; (in Urdū) a glass shade (of a candlestick, &c.)
subs: or fānūs-ě-gardān, or ćarḵẖī fānūs, A Chinese lantern; a lantern into which a light framework with pictures attached thereto is inserted (the framework and pictures are caused to revolve by the smoke of the candle); a phantasmagoria; a magic lantern.

def: s.m. & adv. A small quantity given above the quantity purchased; — over and above, to boot (cf. phāltū; — syn. ghaluʼā, rūkhan, set-kā).
more: s. m. A small quantity given above the quantity purchased, to boot.

def: s.m. A mattock, spade.

n. m. S. फाला A mattock; spade; hoe. Jahāṅ suī nahīṅ jātī, wahāṅ phāoṛā karte haiṅ . Prov. Not room for a needle and he introduces a hatchet. (Said of exaggeration). phā'oṛā se dāṅt . Long projecting teeth.

n. f. 1. Dim. of phāoṛā , q. v. 2. See (بيراگا) 3. A piece of wood or staff with a support at each end, which is held in both hands and laid horizontally, in performing the exercise called ḍaṅḍ . 4. An instrument like a small rake or hoe for removing a horse's dung.

s.f. A shovel, hoe, an instrument like a small rake or hoe for removing a horse's dung; — a crutch on which a jogī leans; — a piece of wood or staff with a support at each end (which is held in both hands and laid horizontally in performing the exercise of ḍanḍ; — syn. bairāgā).
more: s. f. 1. A crutch on which a jogī leans. 2. A piece of wood or staff with a support at each end, which is held in both hands and laid horizontally, in performing the exercise of ḍanḍ. 3. An instrument like a small rake or hoe for removing a horseʼs dung.

def: v.n. = phadphadānā, q.v. v.n. To bubble, to fizz, to sputter; to ferment; to shoot, throw out shoots (as a plant); to break out (as an eruption); to be inflamed (with lust).
more: v. a. See phadphadānā. Purūā bahal, sūkhal ghāv phaphaṅdal . Bhoj. Prov. The East wind blew, the dried up sore broke out anew.

def: n. f. H. phapṛā pretend. A dextrous, cunning, crafty, or artful procuress or bawd; conciliatrix. Māṅ se ziyādah chāhe so phāpā-kuṭnī . Prov. Who more than mother's love profess know for a wheedling procuress.
s.m. A blister, vesicle; a bubble
more: s. m. A blister. پهپهولي پهوٿني phaphole phūṭne, v. n. To be blistered; it is used to express the mind being blistered, to be afflicted. پهپهولي دل کي پهوڙني phaphole dil ke phoṛne, To satisfy a revenge that has long been rankling in oneʼs breast.
subs: v.n. To be blistered; (met.) to be afflicted in mind
v.n. To satisfy a revenge that has long been rankling in one's breast; to give vent to one's passion, appease one's wrath.

def: adj. Swelled, swollen, puffed, inflated, distended, bloated, fat, corpulent, flabby; — insipid, weak, watery (also written phophsā).
s.f. = phaphūṅdī, q.v.
more: s. f. Mould (on fruit, bread, &c. from dampness).

def: s.f. Mouldiness; mould (on fruit, bread, &c. from damp), mildew
subs: v.n. To be mildewed, be mouldy.

def: s.m. A blister ( = phapholā).
s.m. = phapholā, q.v. s.m. A blister, vesicle; a bubble
more: n. m. S. स्फोट. A blister; vesicle. phapholā phūṭnā , v. n. To be blistered. dil ke phapole phoṛ'nā, jale phaphole phoṛnā , v. n. To give vent to one's passion; appease one's rage.

s. m. A blister. پهپهولي پهوٿني phaphole phūṭne, v. n. To be blistered; it is used to express the mind being blistered, to be afflicted. پهپهولي دل کي پهوڙني phaphole dil ke phoṛne, To satisfy a revenge that has long been rankling in oneʼs breast.
subs: v.n. To be blistered; (met.) to be afflicted in mind
v.n. To satisfy a revenge that has long been rankling in one's breast; to give vent to one's passion, appease one's wrath.

def: adj. = phapphas and phaphsā, q.v. adj. Swelled, swollen, puffed, inflated, distended, bloated, fat, corpulent, flabby; — insipid, weak, watery (also written phophsā).
adj. Swelled, swollen, puffed, inflated, distended, bloated, fat, corpulent, flabby; — insipid, weak, watery (also written phophsā).
more: adj. Fat; corpulent; flabby. Moṭā phapphas .

def: s.m. Pretence; hypocritical show.
n. m. A. dallāla , a procuress. Pretence; hypocritical show.
s.m. A layer of mud on a wooden roof.
adj. = phapphas and phaphsā, q.v. adj. Swelled, swollen, puffed, inflated, distended, bloated, fat, corpulent, flabby; — insipid, weak, watery (also written phophsā).
more: adj. 1. Swelled; puffed. 2. Insipid; tasteless. phapsā , n. m. A layer of mud on a wooden roof.

def: s.f. = phaphūṅdī, q.v.
v.n. To be mildewed ( = phaphūṅdī lagnā, q.v.s.v. phaphūṅdī).
s.m. (Prov.) = phal, q.v. s.m. A ploughshare; — an epithet of Mahā-deva, and of Bala-rāma.
more: n. f. 1. A gaming house. 2. A yard where goods are delivered or exposed for sale. 3. The shafts or pole of a carriage. 4. A gun carriage. phaṛ pe rakhnā yā lagānā , v. n. To stake; wager.
s. m. A fruit. See پهل.

def: s.f. A place of public business or of public resort; a place where goods in quantity are exposed for inspection or sale; a mart, exchange, &c.; a gambling-house (esp. where dice are played); — the shafts or pole of a carriage; a gun-carriage; the stand of a great gun
more: s. f. 1. A gaming-house where dice are played. 2. A place where goods are exposed for sale. 3. The shafts or pole of a carriage. پهڙباز phaṛ-bāz, s. m. 1. A player at dice, a gamester. 2. A prater, a talkative fellow. پهڙبازي phaṛ-bāzī, s. f. Gambling, playing at dice.
subs: s.m. A gamester, gambler; swaggerer, prater, talkative fellow
s.f. Gambling, playing at dice; — phaṛ-pe rakhnā, phaṛ-pe lagānā, v.t. To wager, to stake.

def: v. a. To worry.
adj. (dakh.) Ravenous; ravenous beast, &c. see درنده.
s.m. Rent, split, crack, fissure.
adj. (f. -ī), Half-dried, half-dry; dry.
more: 1. s. m. A vane, a pennant. 2. adj. Half-dried.

def: A flag, standard, vane, pennant.
A flag, standard, vane, pennant.
more: 1. s. m. A vane, a pennant. 2. adj. Half-dried.

def: adj. = pharahrā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Half-dried, half-dry; dry.
more: adj. Dry ( khushk ); half dry. Pharairī dāl .

crispy, crunchy

more: n. f. Trembling; shivering ( phurphurī ). phurairī lenā , v. n. To be revived, refreshed.

def: s.m. A dice-player, a gambler ( = phaṛ-bāz).
s.m. A shield ( = phar).
more: n. f. S. स्फुर 1. Fluttering. flutter. 2. Throbbing; palpitation; agitation.

def: s.f. = phaṛak, q.v. s.m. A shield ( = phar).
s.f. Fluttering, flutter, flap; fluctuating; vibrating, quivering, twitching, pulsation, throbbing, palpitating, palpitation; agitation (cf. bharak); weltering (in blood)
more: verbal n. f. (of پهڙکنا) Fluttering, flutter, vibrating, throbbing, palpitating.
subs: adv. After repeated fluttering or fluctuation (between hope and despair).

def: v.n. = phaṛaknā, q.v.
more: v. n. 1. To flutter; to be convulsed (eyelids, muscles, etc). 2. To beat; pulsate; throb; palpitate; go pit-a-pat. 3. To yearn; burn; long; to be dying for. Maiṅ to terī sūrat ko phaṛak gaī . Wom.

def: v.n. To flutter, to twitch convulsively, vibrate with convulsive involuntary motion (as the eyelids, &c.), to quiver, throb, beat, pulsate, palpitate; to writhe (cf. bhaṛaknā); to welter (in blood, as a wounded person) — to yearn or long (for).
more: v. n. To flutter, to vibrate with convulsive involuntary motion, as the eyelids and other muscles; to throb, to palpitate; to writhe (the shoulders, &c.).

def: v.t. To cause to be torn, split, or cleft, &c. (cf. phaṭānā).
more: ( स्फाटन or caus. of پهاڙنا q. v.) v. a. To cause to be torn, split or cleaved.

def: s.m. = pharrās, q.v. s.m. A species of fir (so called from the whistling or whizzing of its branches in the wind).
s.f. Wrangling, squabbling; squabble, quarrel, fight.
more: s. f. Quarrel, squabble, battle, fight: see جنگ jang.

def: s. (dakh.) Blade (of a sword: Harris ).
